messages.content: Several risk write-ups may describe the same underlying risk. Pick the best exemplar (sharpest mechanism, best evidence) and fold any UNIQUE early-warning indicators from the others into it. If they are genuinely different risks, keep the strongest as exemplar anyway only when they truly overlap — otherwise return the highest-scored id with no folded indicators and note they are distinct.

content: {"keepId":"risk.execution-window.som-window-semantics-mismatch","rationale":"This cluster contains two genuinely distinct risk families that happen to all touch obtainableFactor: (1) a SOM-window-semantics / Year-1-capture-overstatement family, and (2) a demand-timing / grid-and-prelet-delivery-trough family, plus adjacent competitive-foreclosure and compliance-schedule risks. The largest true-overlap sub-cluster is the SOM-window-semantics group: som-window-semantics-mismatch (18), yam-window-is-a-qualification-corridor (17), and year1-obtainable-correlated-with-slowest-cell (17) all make the identical core claim — the generic 1-5% SOM band describes a multi-year (1-3/3-5yr) capture window but is applied to a 12-month pre-launch year, so 0.03 overstates Year-1 capture ~5-10x, evidenced by the same TryBuildCo (~0.1%) and Tractian (~0.001%) analogues. som-window-semantics-mismatch is the sharpest and highest-scored exemplar of this definitional mismatch (cleanest mechanism, tightest to the benchmark-source semantics). I fold in the unique early-warning indicators from the two overlapping siblings (qualification-milestone slippage, POC-to-PO conversion, large-operator qualification stage-gates, TED award-cycle timing) that the exemplar lacks. I do NOT fold the grid/pre-let demand-discontinuity risks (grid-queue, enefg, prelet-lockout), the competitive-foreclosure risks (bundling, distributor lockout, fragmented incumbency), or the compliance-schedule/CRA/firmware risks — those name genuinely different mechanisms (delivery timing, channel access, competitive response, cert-schedule serialization) even though several also route to obtainableFactor.","foldedIndicators":[{"signal":"Certification milestone slippage vs the launch plan (full compliance stack completion)","where":"Notified-body/TÜV test scheduling and the venture's own conformity-assessment tracker","threshold":"Full compliance stack not complete by month 6 post-kickoff","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"POC-to-PO conversion rate and elapsed cycle time on the first five pilots","where":"Internal pipeline reviews / POC acceptance sign-offs at pilot accounts","threshold":"Median pilot-to-PO elapsed time >6 months or acceptance rate <70%","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Elapsed time and stage-gates in the venture's first large-operator qualification processes","where":"Own CRM pipeline plus operator supplier-registration portals (Equinix/DLR vendor onboarding)","threshold":"First qualification not complete within 6 months of launch","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"DC fit-out and framework tender award cycles for rack power in CE","where":"TED (tenders.europa.eu) award notices vs publication dates for data-centre electrical packages","threshold":"Median publication-to-award >9 months","updates":"increases"}]}
